versionNotes: Sprint 4 W13 Goal Statement — no-AI form activity, path choice + 5 required open-ended fields + conditional pair-merge details + rejection warning + 4 confirmation checkboxes
title: Sprint 4 Goal Statement
description: Set your Sprint 4 direction — solo continue or pair-merge — grounded in what Sprint 3 taught you.

questions.infoBlock:
Section 1 of 9 · Choose your path

How are you approaching Sprint 4? Pick one of the two paths below and type it as your answer.

Solo continue

I'm staying with my current problem and building deeper on my Sprint 3 work.

Pair-merge

I'm combining forces with a classmate to tackle a shared problem together.

If you want to switch to an entirely new problem, talk to the instructor first. That path exists but needs a conversation.
questions.prompt: Type either "Solo continue" or "Pair-merge" — exactly as written above.

questions.infoBlock:
Section 2 of 9 · The foundation

What did you ship or demonstrate in Sprint 3? Link to your Solution Brief if helpful.

Review your Sprint 3 Solution Brief: S3: Solution Brief
questions.prompt: What did you ship or demonstrate in Sprint 3? Link to your Solution Brief if helpful.
questions.placeholder: Describe your Sprint 3 deliverable and what state it's in now...

questions.infoBlock:
Section 3 of 9 · The lessons

What did you learn — about your problem, your approach, or yourself as a builder — that changes how you'll work in Sprint 4?
questions.prompt: What did you learn — about your problem, your approach, or yourself as a builder — that changes how you'll work in Sprint 4?
questions.placeholder: Be specific about what shifted in your understanding...

questions.infoBlock:
Section 4 of 9 · The goal

What is the specific, incremental goal you're setting for Sprint 4? This should build meaningfully on Sprint 3 — not repeat it, not ignore it.
questions.prompt: What is the specific, incremental goal you're setting for Sprint 4? This should build meaningfully on Sprint 3 — not repeat it, not ignore it.
questions.placeholder: State your goal clearly enough that someone else could tell whether you achieved it...

questions.infoBlock:
Section 5 of 9 · The why

Why is this the right next step? What makes it meaningful — to the problem, to your learning, or to the people affected?
questions.prompt: Why is this the right next step? What makes it meaningful — to the problem, to your learning, or to the people affected?
questions.placeholder: Explain what makes this goal worth pursuing right now...

questions.infoBlock:
Section 6 of 9 · What done looks like

Describe what "done" looks like for this goal. What will exist at the end of Sprint 4 that doesn't exist now?
questions.prompt: Describe what 'done' looks like for this goal. What will exist at the end of Sprint 4 that doesn't exist now?
questions.placeholder: Be concrete — what artifact, demo, or evidence will you produce?

questions.infoBlock:
Section 7 of 9 · Pair-merge details

Only fill this out if you chose Pair-merge in Section 1.

If you chose Solo continue, type "N/A — going solo" and move on.
questions.prompt: Who is your partner, and what does the merge look like? How are you combining your Sprint 3 work?
questions.placeholder: Name your partner and describe how the two projects connect... (or type "N/A — going solo")

questions.infoBlock:
Section 8 of 9 · Before you submit

⚠️ Your goal statement can be sent back for revision

Common reasons:

  • The goal is too vague to evaluate
  • It doesn't connect to Sprint 3 work
  • "Done" isn't concrete enough

If that happens, you'll get specific feedback and a chance to resubmit.
questions.prompt: Read the warning above before continuing to the confirmations.

questions.infoBlock:
Section 9 of 9 · Confirmations

Confirm each statement below by typing "Yes" next to it. All four are required.

1. I have reviewed my Sprint 3 Solution Brief before writing this goal.

2. My goal is specific enough that someone else could tell whether I achieved it.

3. I've explained how this builds on (not repeats) my Sprint 3 work.

4. I understand my goal statement may be returned for revision.
questions.prompt:
Type your four confirmations in this format:
1. Yes
2. Yes
3. Yes
4. Yes
questions.placeholder:
1. Yes — I have reviewed my Sprint 3 Solution Brief before writing this goal.
2. Yes — My goal is specific enough that someone else could tell whether I achieved it.
3. Yes — I've explained how this builds on (not repeats) my Sprint 3 work.
4. Yes — I understand my goal statement may be returned for revision.
